_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description:
You can:
- ensure digital, data and technology shapes the organisation’s strategy
- lead digital, data and technology thinking, processes and practices, and integrate them into organisational decision making
- ensure digital, data and technology meets user needs and enables the organisation's outcomes
Examples of leadership using this skill:
- creating partnerships across the organisation to build trust as a recognised strategic leader
- increasing understanding amongst senior leaders so that everyone shares the value of using digital, data and technology to shape the organisation’s strategy
- rooting conversations on the organisation’s strategy in user needs and challenging strategic decisions made without understanding the user's context
reference_document_element_description: Skill in communicating data quality and data trust issues to non-technical executive stakeholders in terms of business risk and operational impact.
comments: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI DQ domain. DQ-S-009 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-T-010 (LS, cosine: 0.536), OP-A-003 (OP, cosine: 0.516).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.1/10 (raw 0.612). Shared vocabulary: [data, skill, trust]. DDaT-only terms [across, amongst, build, challenging, context, conversations] vs WIDAI-only terms [business, communicating, executive, impact, issues, non] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.838 (P=0.820, R=0.857). R exceeds P by 0.037 — the WIDAI KSA's 21 words are well-captured by the DDaT element's 106 words, but the DDaT element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance:
NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "You can:
- ensure digital, data and technology shapes the organisation’s strateg" and "Skill in communicating data quality and data trust issues to non-technical execu" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.538 < STS 0.612 (gap -0.074). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [across, amongst, build] and [business, communicating, executive] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 9.2%. Shared tokens: [data, skill, trust]. DDaT-only: [across, amongst, build, challenging, context, conversations]. WIDAI-only: [business, communicating, executive, impact, issues, non].
